title: Innodata launches federal business unit to serve US government AI needs
text: Innodata Inc. (NASDAQ:INOD) established Innodata Federal, a dedicated business unit targeting defense, intelligence, and civilian government agencies. The division will provide AI data engineering, generative AI, and agentic AI solutions. The company has already secured partnerships with defense technology firms and received a direct award from a defense agency. CEO Jack Abuhoff stated: Innodata Federal represents a strategic investment in a high-growth market where we believe our capabilities align exceptionally well with government modernization priorities. Service offerings include: AI data engineering for computer vision and language systems. Generative AI solutions for model fine-tuning. Agentic AI for workflow automation. Model evaluation and document intelligence services. Open-source intelligence capabilities. Led by Senior Vice President Vinay Malkani, the unit emphasizes rapid deployment, a STEM workforce with security clearances, and enterprise-scale infrastructure. The announcement coincided with earnings results and the appointment of retired four-star Army General Richard D. Clarke to the board. Innodata plans to leverage government procurement pathways including Other Transaction Authorities and Commercial Solutions Openings. The company currently serves five Magnificent Seven tech companies and numerous Fortune 1000 enterprises.
